failure_surface: Test case validation failed despite appearing correct logic - likely due to incorrect row indexing or missing header row consideration
root_cause: Agent assumed all rows contain data starting from row 1, but spreadsheet likely contains a header row that needs to be skipped during data processing. The verification prints show Row 1 containing data, suggesting no header offset was applied, which caused misalignment between expected answer positions (B1:B4) and actual data location.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before iterating through rows, detect whether row 1 contains a header (non-data content like column names). If a header exists, adjust the data processing loop to start from row 2 instead of row 1, ensuring output is written to the correct logical rows.
evidence: Test case result was 0 (failure) despite clean execution without errors
Verification prints showed data being written to rows 1-4 directly without checking for header presence
Task specified answer_position B1:B4 but did not clarify if these were data rows or included header row
No explicit header detection logic in the solution code

memory_items.title: Header Row Detection Before Data Processing
memory_items.lesson: Always check whether the first row of a spreadsheet contains column headers before assuming row 1 is data.
memory_items.generalization: When loading spreadsheets programmatically, examine the content type of the first row (text-like headers vs. data patterns). If a header row exists, skip it during data iteration and offset all row indices accordingly.

memory_items.title: Verify Cell Range Alignment with Instruction Semantics
memory_items.lesson: Answer position specifications may refer to logical positions in the data table rather than absolute Excel row numbers.
memory_items.generalization: Cross-reference the answer_position specification with detected sheet structure. If headers exist, subtract 1 from target row numbers; if no headers exist, use absolute row positions as specified.

memory_items.title: Validate Output Against Expected Row Mapping
memory_items.lesson: After writing results, verify that output rows correspond to the correct source data rows using schema checks.
memory_items.generalization: Include a post-processing validation step that confirms the number of populated output rows matches the expected count based on detected data boundaries, not just total worksheet rows.

edits.content:
### Header Detection Protocol
- Before processing data rows, inspect row 1 for header indicators: mixed text patterns, capitalized words, or non-numeric content typical of labels.
- If header detected, set data_start_row = 2; otherwise, data_start_row = 1.
- Adjust all row iteration ranges using this calculated offset.
- When answer_position specifies B1:B4, map this to actual data rows accounting for any header offset.

### Row Indexing Validation
- After completing modifications, compare the count of modified cells against the expected delta from input data rows.
- Include a sanity-check routine that validates output rows correspond to their intended input source rows by spot-checking original values alongside transformed values.
